name: Shrimp & Barbecued Pork Fried Rice
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Cooked long-grain rice, preferably cold
1/2 cup Chinese barbecued pork, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
1/2 cup Leftover cooked chicken, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
1/2 cup Fresh or frozen peas, blanched
1 cup Finely shredded romaine (or Iceberg lettuce)
recipeInstructions:
1. Break up clumps of rice by gently rubbing between the palms of your hands and transfer to a large bowl.
2. Over medium-high heat, preheat wok until hot. Add oil; tilt wok to coat sides. When oil is moderately hot, add salt and optional shrimp paste, stirring for 5 seconds until fragrant.
3. Immediately add rice and quickly stir-fry, pressing and poking at clumps of rice until all grains are separated without browning the rice (about 3 minutes).
4. Season with sugar, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. Stir-fry until each grain is evenly coated (about 1 minute).
5. Push rice to sides of wok. Add beaten egg mixture to center of wok and allow to cook, lightly beating eggs in the center only (about 1 minute). Small flecks of egg will appear interspersed in the rice.
6. Toss together with rice. Add shrimp, barbecued pork, chicken, peas, lettuce, and green onions; toss and stir until mixed and heated through and lettuce is wilted (about 2 minutes).
